title: Cisco stock pops 15% on 4,000 layoffs and an AI order surge
desc: Daily AI news satire. Cisco cuts ~4,000 jobs, books $5.3B in AI infrastructure orders, raises guidance to $9B — and lays off the engineers who built the networks.

beats.name: temple-cisco-cold-open
beats.narration: Cisco announced its largest restructuring in years on Wednesday.

beats.name: earnings-scroll-unfurls
beats.narration: The company will cut close to four thousand jobs this quarter.

beats.name: workers-file-out
beats.narration: That is just under five percent of its global workforce.

beats.name: empty-temple-wide
beats.narration: The layoffs are part of a deeper pivot into A.I. infrastructure.

beats.name: shrine-pulses-brighter
beats.narration: Per CNBC, Cisco's stock rose roughly fifteen percent on the news of the cuts.

beats.name: stock-ticker-climbs-wall
beats.narration: The company also reported record quarterly revenue of fifteen point eight billion dollars.

beats.name: ingot-pile-grows
beats.narration: Cisco said it has booked five point three billion dollars in A.I. infrastructure orders this fiscal year.

beats.name: guidance-scroll-raised
beats.narration: It raised its annual A.I. order guidance from five billion to nine billion dollars.

beats.name: ceo-silhouette-blesses-shrine
beats.narration: Chief Executive Chuck Robbins said in a blog post that becoming a winner in A.I. meant making hard decisions.

beats.name: blog-post-scroll-publish
beats.narration: The blog post went up the same day the cuts were announced.

beats.name: titans-bring-monoliths
beats.narration: Per the company, the order surge is driven by its hyperscaler customers — the firms running the largest A.I. data centers.

beats.name: shrine-reads-layoff-list
beats.narration: The workers being let go are largely the ones who built the networks those data centers run on.

sources.attribution: Cisco's stock pops 15% on surging AI orders, as company says it's cutting almost 4,000 jobs (CNBC)
Cisco layoffs loom as company pivots deeper into AI after strong quarter (Fox Business)
Workforce Cuts Under 5% as Company Pivots to AI and Posts Record Revenue (IndexBox)

transcript: Cisco announced its largest restructuring in years on Wednesday. The company will cut close to four thousand jobs this quarter. That is just under five percent of its global workforce. The layoffs are part of a deeper pivot into A.I. infrastructure. Per CNBC, Cisco's stock rose roughly fifteen percent on the news of the cuts. The company also reported record quarterly revenue of fifteen point eight billion dollars. Cisco said it has booked five point three billion dollars in A.I. infrastructure orders this fiscal year. It raised its annual A.I. order guidance from five billion to nine billion dollars. Chief Executive Chuck Robbins said in a blog post that becoming a winner in A.I. meant making hard decisions. The blog post went up the same day the cuts were announced. Per the company, the order surge is driven by its hyperscaler customers — the firms running the largest A.I. data centers. The workers being let go are largely the ones who built the networks those data centers run on.
